name: Mitigation
text: While it is not denied that '{x}' {xy_relation} a {y_polarity} outcome '{y}', the causal relationship can be mitigated through the means of '{z}'.
cqs: If '{z}' mitigates but does not completely eliminate the effects, can the remaining impacts be ignored?
Is '{z}' an appropriate way to mitigate the causal relationship between '{x}' and '{y}'?

name: Alternative
text: While it is not denied that '{x}' {xy_relation} a {y_polarity} outcome '{y}', another factor '{z}' also {zy_relation} '{y}', and '{z}' should be prioritized over '{x}'.
cqs: If both ‘{x}’ and ‘{z}’ {xy_relation} ‘{y}’, why should ‘{z}’ be prioritized over ‘{x}’?
Why do you think ‘{z}’ {xy_relation} ‘{y}’?

name: No evidence
text: There is no evidence to support that '{x}' {xy_relation} '{y}'.
cqs: To consider counterarguments from various perspectives, let’s also explore patterns other than ‘No evidence’. Can you think of a counter-argument with another structure?

name: Another True Cause
text: '{x}' does not {xy_relation} '{y}', instead, '{z}' {zy_relation} '{y}'; thus we should not focus on '{x}'
cqs: Why do you think '{x}' does not {xy_relation} '{y}'?
Why do you think '{z}' {zy_relation} '{y}'?

name: Missing mechanism #1
text: '{x}' {trans1_former} another factor '{z}', and '{z}' {trans1_latter} '{y}'. Therefore, '{x}' does not {xy_relation} '{y}' but rather {xy_relation_reverse} '{y}'.
cqs: Why do you think ‘{x}’ {xy_relation_reverse} ‘{y}’ through ‘{z}’ to a greater extent than it {xy_relation} ‘{y}’?
Why do you think ‘{x}’ {trans1_former} ‘{z}’?
Why do you think ‘{z}’ {trans1_latter} ‘{y}’?

name: Missing mechanism #2
text: '{x}' {trans2_former} another factor '{z}', and '{z}' {trans2_latter} '{y}'. Therefore, '{x}' does not {xy_relation} '{y}' but rather {xy_relation_reverse} '{y}'.
cqs: Why do you think ‘{x}’ {xy_relation_reverse} ‘{y}’ through ‘{z}’ to a greater extent than it {xy_relation} ‘{y}’?
Why do you think ‘{x}’ {trans2_former} ‘{z}’?
Why do you think ‘{z}’ {trans2_latter} ‘{y}’?

name: No need to address
text: '{y}' is sufficient as it is, or '{y}' is not important so there is no need to take action for '{y}'.
cqs: Can the negative impact of taking no action be ignored?

name: Negative effect due to y #1
text: Since '{y}' {yz_relation_y_pro_z} a {z_polarity_y_pro_z} outcome '{z}', '{y}' is not a {y_polarity} outcome.
cqs: Why is the {ptn81_cq_former} of ‘{z}’ more significant than the {ptn81_cq_latter} of ‘{y}’?
Why do you think ‘{y}’ {yz_relation_y_pro_z} ‘{z}’?

name: Negative effect due to y #2
text: Since '{y}' {yz_relation_y_sup_z} a {z_polarity_y_sup_z} outcome '{z}', '{y}' is not a {y_polarity} outcome.
cqs: Why is the {ptn82_cq_former} of suppressing ‘{z}’ more significant than the {ptn82_cq_latter} of ‘{y}’?

name: Positive effects of a different perspective from y #1
text: '{x}' promotes a {z_polarity_x_pro_z} outcome '{z}', which is a different perspective from '{y}'
cqs: Why is the {ptn9_cq_former} of promoting ‘{z}’ more significant than {ptn9_cq_latter} of {xy_relation} ‘{y}’?
